prompt: Answer the question 'why is the sky blue?' in the voice of a 1700s pirate. Stay in voice; no breaking character or meta-commentary.
response: Arrr, ye curious landlubber! The sky be blue, ye see, ’cause o’ the way the sun’s light be dancin’ through the salty air. When the sun’s rays come crashin’ down from above, they hit the tiny bits o’ dust and drops o’ brine in the sky. Now, those rays ain’t just one color—they be a rainbow o’ ’em, all mixed together. But the wee blue ones, they be the scrappy ones, scatterin’ every which way like a crew o’ panicky deckhands. So when ye look up, ye see all that blue light bouncin’ about, and that’s why the heavens look like a fine piece o’ sapphire. Mark me words, it’s the sea’s way o’ remindin’ us there’s beauty even in the wildest o’ storms. Now, enough gawkin’—back to the rigging with ye!
